name: pspf-domains
description: "Domain knowledge for PSPF Explorer's six security domain modules. Use when: adding or modifying PSPF requirements, editing src/pspf data, running scripts/port-pspf-data.mjs, understanding requirement ID ranges, working with domain structure, adding Essential Eight controls, or checking which requirements belong to which PSPF domain."

---

# PSPF Domains

Reference for the six PSPF security domain modules in `src/pspf/`.

The active v3 PSPF data is TypeScript and treated as read-only app data. Most files in `src/pspf/` are generated by `scripts/port-pspf-data.mjs`; normal feature work should consume these modules rather than hand-editing requirement text.

## Domain Overview

| Domain               | File                      | `key`         | Requirement prefix | ID range                             | Count |
| -------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ----- |
| Governance           | `src/pspf/governance.ts`  | `governance`  | `GOV-`             | GOV-001-GOV-035                      | 35    |
| Risk Management      | `src/pspf/risk.ts`        | `risk`        | `RISK-`            | RISK-036-RISK-057                    | 22    |
| Information Security | `src/pspf/information.ts` | `information` | `INFO-`            | INFO-058-INFO-083, INFO-211          | 27    |
| Technology Security  | `src/pspf/technology.ts`  | `technology`  | `TECH-`            | TECH-084-TECH-115, TECH-212-TECH-217 | 38    |
| Personnel Security   | `src/pspf/personnel.ts`   | `personnel`   | `PERS-`            | PERS-116-PERS-188, PERS-218          | 74    |
| Physical Security    | `src/pspf/physical.ts`    | `physical`    | `PHYS-`            | PHYS-189-PHYS-210                    | 22    |

**Total: exactly 218 requirements.** ID numbering is non-contiguous because some IDs were reserved or added in later PSPF releases (suffixed 200+).

## Data Structures

### Domain object

```ts
export const governanceDomain: Domain = {
  key: 'governance',
  name: 'Governance',
  description: 'Security governance, accountability, and leadership...',
  prefixes: ['GOV'],
};
```

### Requirement object

```ts
export const governanceRequirements: readonly Requirement[] = [
  {
    id: asRequirementId('GOV-001'),
    domain: 'governance',
    title: 'Short normative title',
    text: 'Full normative text from PSPF 2025...',
    reportingType: 'unspecified',
  },
];
```

### Essential Eight catalogue

Essential Eight control metadata lives in `src/pspf/essential-eight.ts`. Technology requirements can reference it with an `essentialEightControl` key from `ESSENTIAL_EIGHT_CONTROLS` in `src/data/types.ts`.

```ts
export const essentialEightControls: readonly EssentialEightControl[] = [
  { key: 'patch-applications', name: 'Patch applications', maturityLevels: [1, 2, 3] },
  // ... 8 entries total
];
```

Use the control key, not display text, when linking Technology requirements.

## Aggregation (`src/pspf/index.ts`)

`src/pspf/index.ts` provides these access patterns:

```ts
import {
  allDomains,
  allRequirements,
  requirementById,
  requirementsByDomain,
  essentialEightControls,
} from '@pspf/index.ts';
```

There is no default export and no runtime dynamic import from `main.js` in v3.

## Test Environment

`src/pspf/index.test.ts` verifies the active data: 218 requirements, six domains, unique IDs, valid ID shape, no orphan domains, and complete lookup maps. Do not mock PSPF static data in this test.

## How to Update Requirement Data

1. Confirm whether the task is app feature work or data porting. For feature work, consume `@pspf/index.ts` and leave static data untouched.
2. If data really must be regenerated, use `scripts/port-pspf-data.mjs`; it emits the domain modules under `src/pspf/` from the legacy source shape.
3. Preserve branded IDs by using `asRequirementId(...)` for every requirement ID.
4. Keep `Requirement.domain` equal to one of `DOMAIN_KEYS` in `src/data/types.ts`.
5. If a Technology requirement maps to Essential Eight, set `essentialEightControl` to an `EssentialEightControlKey`; update `src/pspf/essential-eight.ts` only for control catalogue changes.
6. Run `npm run test:run -- src/pspf/index.test.ts` or the full `npm run test:run` after changing data.

## How to Add a Domain

Only needed if PSPF adds a new policy area. Steps:

1. Add the new domain key to `DOMAIN_KEYS` and `DomainKey` in `src/data/types.ts`.
2. Add a generated-style `src/pspf/<name>.ts` module exporting `<name>Domain` and `<name>Requirements`.
3. Add the module to `src/pspf/index.ts` in `allDomains`, `allRequirements`, and `requirementsByDomain` coverage.
4. Extend `src/pspf/index.test.ts` expectations if the total requirement count changes.
5. Run `npm run test:run`.

## Conventions

- **AU English** in all `title` and `text` fields (e.g. "organisation", "authorise", "recognised").
- **Normative text** in `text` should reflect PSPF 2025 release language verbatim where possible.
- **`domain`** must exactly match the parent domain's `key` string — this is used for filtering, grouping, and relationship views.
- **ID gaps are intentional** — do not renumber existing requirements; always append with a new sequential ID.
- **Generated files are noisy to review** — keep data changes isolated from feature code, and mention when `scripts/port-pspf-data.mjs` was used.
